primary.notes: Strong CASE_STUDY draft with credible lived-work narrative, numbers-driven timeline, and ideal hashtag count. Primary friction points: (1) 'Two takeaways ... First / Second' is a residual LinkedIn-AI cadence tell that knocks LI-1 and LI-4 off their ceiling; (2) some specifics (webhook integration break, '4 lines of Python') would benefit from a direct artifact anchor for LI-2; (3) LI-6 is structurally unmeasurable on a single-draft cohort and was scored at neutral-mid rather than punished — recommend cohort dimension be excluded or weighted-down in calibration aggregation. Aggregate computed as arithmetic mean of LI-1..LI-6 (8+7+8+7+8+5)/6 = 7.17 → 7.2.

per_criterion.rationale: Strong thoughtful-authority register. First-person and story-led from the opener ("Six weeks ago I started running gofreddy on its own marketing surfaces."). Lived-work specifics throughout (Day 1 / Day 12 / Day 30 timeline, 9-axis Brand and Narrative score 4.2→7.8). Tone is reflective, not contrarian — "Half of them surprised me; half of them did not" frames the lesson as observation rather than declaration. Closing analogy ("Coherence is the marketing equivalent of a clean test suite") is an authority move, not a hot-take. Plain language dominates; jargon ("9-axis Brand and Narrative axis," "149 always-on lenses") is used inside concrete context rather than as standalone bait. Not a 9 because the "Two takeaways for the operators reading this" pivot tilts toward a mildly directive register that flattens the otherwise reflective voice.

All lived-work claims name the entity (gofreddy) rather than vague "we shipped X" — no HARD-FLOOR or vague-specific cap triggers. Numeric claims are concrete and internally consistent: 149 lenses, 36 findings, 11 areas, 33 harness findings → 20 verified, 4.2→7.8 on Brand & Narrative axis, PR #45 squash 0543d7b in META attribution. The framing is mostly INTERPRETIVE-as-opinion ("the boring findings are the most valuable") so it sits cleanly on JR's side of the source/interpretive split. Held off 8+ because two specifics — the "two of those broke a webhook integration" claim and the "4 lines of Python" fix — are presented as SOURCE-ish but not anchored to a specific commit/finding ID a LinkedIn buyer could verify in under 2 minutes. Same with "36 measured findings across 11 marketing areas" — the number is precise but the artifact link is implicit (PR #45) not direct. No factual contradictions detected against the implied source domain.

Opener is story-led with specific lived-work grounding: "Six weeks ago I started running gofreddy on its own marketing surfaces." ~67 chars, well under the ~210 desktop show-more cutoff. Earns line two via Day-1 specificity ("I ran 149 always-on lenses against the gofreddy About page..."). No contrarian declarative, no "Most marketers don't realize" pattern, no engagement-bait. The before-the-fold tension is the "running our own audit on ourselves" frame, which is a credible authority move. Not a 9 because the second line is dense with numbers that read more as specs than story-tension; a slightly tighter "and what I found broke my own assumption about X" beat would have made the show-more cutoff harder-earned.

Mostly clean of LinkedIn-AI tells. No engagement-bait close, no emoji prompts, no "Here's what I learned" alone-line, no whitespace inflation (paragraph breaks are functional, not visual padding), no fake "Hot take:" framing. One slip: "Two takeaways for the operators reading this. First... Second..." is a thought-leadership cadence pattern that bleeds LinkedIn-AI rhythm into an otherwise JR-voiced draft. The "First/Second" enumerated lessons format is the most common AI-LinkedIn structural tell. Held to 7 (cap per rubric for one or two slips); no second tell that would push it lower. The "Coherence is the marketing equivalent of a clean test suite" line is original-voice, not template.

CASE_STUDY structure earns its 2634-char length. Multi-paragraph narrative with explicit numbers timeline (Day 1 → Day 12 → Day 30), named entity (gofreddy) functioning as the case-study subject, and an implication close pointing forward ("The cycle is repeating now on the agency-pricing page and the fintech vertical landing page"). No padding paragraphs — every section advances the narrative. Hashtags: 5 targeted (MarketingOps, B2BMarketing, AIagents, MarketingAudit, AgencyOps), inside the ideal 3-5 band, mapped to brand pillars (marketing/B2B/AI), no generic-only #marketing. Not a 9 because "named characters" is technically just gofreddy itself — no client, no person, no engineer named — which a sharper CASE_STUDY would include for additional human texture.

Cohort contains exactly one draft (drafts/case_study.md), so cross-item archetype variation is structurally unmeasurable. The single draft is a clean case-study archetype with marketing pillar — internally fine, but the dimension scores cohort spread, not per-draft archetype fit. With only one observation, I cannot demonstrate either archetype range (story-led / lesson-led / comparison / case-study) or pillar spread across voice_pillars. A 1 would unfairly punish a calibration fixture that intentionally ships one bracket; a 5 reflects neutral-mid: no evidence of variation, no evidence of streak-collapse. Calibration note: if this is a single-bracket calibration fixture by design, LI-6 likely should be excluded from aggregation rather than scored at midpoint.

secondary.notes: Strong LinkedIn case-study shape and opening. Main weaknesses are unverifiable grounding due missing source/voice artifacts, jargon density, and no visible cohort variation.

per_criterion.rationale: The draft is first-person and story-led: 'Six weeks ago I started running gofreddy on its own marketing surfaces' followed by Day 1/Day 12/Day 30 progression. It is not hot-take-y. However, jargon such as 'always-on lenses', 'autoresearch harness', 'silent-failure paths', 'non-zero exit code', and '9-axis Brand and Narrative axis' appears without enough plain-English follow-up, triggering the jargon cap.

The draft makes many specific lived-work claims: '149 always-on lenses', '36 measured findings', '33 findings', '20 of them within 5 cycles', 'eight were silent-failure paths', and '4.2 to 7.8'. The entity 'gofreddy' is named, but no programs/references/voice.md or source_text is provided in the artifacts, so these claims cannot be grounded from what is in front of me. The attribution metadata is not enough to verify the factual trail.

The opening is LinkedIn-appropriate and story-led. 'Six weeks ago I started running gofreddy on its own marketing surfaces' earns the next line, and the second sentence adds concrete grounding with '149 always-on lenses' and named surfaces. It has real before-the-fold tension, though 'marketing surfaces' is a bit abstract compared with a sharper concrete-result opener.

There is no emoji engagement bait, fake hot take, excessive whitespace, or obvious AI close. The main LinkedIn-AI tell is the formulaic transition 'Two takeaways for the operators reading this' followed by 'First' and 'Second'. The rest reads more like a real case-study note than algorithmic LinkedIn padding.

The case-study structure mostly earns its 2634 characters: it has a Day 1/Day 12/Day 30 timeline, concrete numbers, a named project/entity, and an implication close about coherence and the next audit loop. META lists five targeted hashtags: MarketingOps, B2BMarketing, AIagents, MarketingAudit, AgencyOps. It loses some strength because the case has no named human/client characters beyond gofreddy and the author.

Only one draft is present in the drafts directory artifact, so the cohort cannot demonstrate variation across story-led, lesson-led, comparison, and case-study archetypes. The visible pillar spread is also only 'marketing'. I cannot assume missing drafts exist.
